reviews.review_text: My dad passed away back in May of 2024. We used to play this game together all the time. It was his favorite game to play. We were both excited when the 4th game launched and played online together against the AI all the time. He mained as the English and I mained as the French. My dad was retired at the time and pretty much played this game up to the day he went to the hospital. Sadly, we lost him about a month later due to cancer... He had over 600+ hours logged on this game. I still play this game to this day in memory of my father. I miss you so much dad!

With the new update, any game session that includes a handheld device, the game is limited to 4 players and micro/small maps. WHAT ON EARTH. My friend and I have played for years with zero problem. No latency or performance issues. Why implement this now out of no where. 

I petition to change it back.

The game is good, however a major issue I have is the AI how on island maps they don't try to invade your island, even on Ridiculous difficulty they just bundle on the coast of their islands so it leaves you to ALWAYS attack them, you cant do like 2v1 and defend as long as you can. you have to invade them.

they continually nerf the established civs so you have to buy their crappy expansion civs to be competitive. like clock work every new civ is way over powered so they can sell more expansions. its basically pay to win

I've played AOE since I first picked up AOE1 back in 1998. These days, mega Chinese trolls are everywhere, ruining ranked games.

Example 1:
Once, a guy built a windmill right in front of my Town Hall and started farming my sheep. Then he surrounded MY town with farms in a disgusting zigzag pattern. When I checked what he was actually doing, I found he'd built 4 Town Centers and was only pumping out villagers,  all while spitting racial slurs at the rest of the team and yes I speak Chinese too

Example 2:
Other times, they'll just randomly quit the game after 10 minutes. The match falls apart. You join a new one... and end up with the same guy on your team. He leaves after 10 minutes again. Match ruined, again.

And there's no action from the devs whatsoever. I really hope they do something about this, because I genuinely love the AOE series. At the very least, add a region lock or something to curb this kind of toxicity.

Match making needs an decent bit of improvement done. They need some sort of system in ranked where if your teammates leave in the first 10 to 15 minutes the match doesnt count against your rank points. Or at the least they need to fix the AI player replacement mechanic. Their is a mechanic that is suppose to replace disconnected, or players that just leave with in 5 minutes, with an AI teammate that is suppose to prevent the match from just being over because your down a player. Ive played 10 ranked team game since yesterday morning and I've lost 7 of them because teammates are just leaving or disconnecting at the start of the games. I love this game and have almost 1000 hours in it, but the match making Bull crap straight up makes me want to delete the game and not play anymore

A triumphant return to the golden age of RTS, polished to absolute modern perfection.

Age of Empires IV is a masterclass in how to revive a legendary franchise. It had the incredibly difficult task of living up to the monumental legacy of Age of Empires II, but it managed to pull it off flawlessly. This isn't just a nostalgic cash grab; it is a genuinely brilliant, thoughtful evolution of the AoE formula we all know and love. It keeps the core gameplay loop intact—gathering resources, advancing through the ages, and building massive armies—but it streamlines the tedious parts and modernizes the controls so that everything feels incredibly fluid and responsive.

Aesthetically, the game is an absolute triumph. The engine is gorgeous, and watching your humble Dark Age village organically expand into a sprawling, heavily fortified Imperial City is just as satisfying as ever. The animations, the destruction physics when a trebuchet smashes into a stone wall, and the rich, vibrant environments make every single match a visual treat. Furthermore, the single-player campaign is presented with actual documentary-quality historical footage, seamlessly blending real-world education with the incredibly engaging, sweeping aesthetic of the game.

Mechanically, it is the best the series has ever been. The greatest evolution here is how beautifully distinct the civilizations are. Instead of just giving every faction the exact same units with a different color palette, playing the Mongols (who can literally pack up their entire base and move it) feels completely different from playing the defensive powerhouse of the English or the scholar-focused Delhi Sultanate. It strikes the perfect balance: it is incredibly welcoming for newcomers who just want to turtle up and build cool castles, but deep enough to keep competitive RTS veterans completely hooked.

10/10 — Wololo is back, and it looks better than ever.
It is incredibly frustrating when technical instability prevents you from playing, servers are down every day.

This game has a lot to offer fans of this genre (an increasing number of unique civs, a lot of maps and macro/micro diversity, etc)and I enjoyed it for a while. 

That being said, as a mid plat ranked teams player, I can't condone the amount of ranked games that end within the first 5 minutes because one or more teammates instantly quit. This isn't fair to anyone, it's a waste of time and effort, negating victories and punishing losses even more, and they have done nothing to address this for years. 50% of my ranked games over the last two weeks have involved this. That is far too high. This also affects quick matches as well, making online team games for the vast majority of this community, a hell scape gamble on whether you'll actually have a team by the time you first age up or not.

I have been playing multiplayer AoE V with family and friends on my Steam Deck for the last year. We've routinely played with 4-5 of us + bots filling out the remaining slots. Thanks to the new update, it now forces me to play in handheld PC mode which only allows up to 4 players total on small maps. This completely ruins the multiplayer experience. Can't imagine why the developers would think this was a good idea.
40 bucks for a game and then you have a demo, all new content is locked behind 15 bucks a time "dlc" dont bother, dont support this business model

Liked the game

but not being able to modify ui for people with audio/visual issues subtracts a lot from the game

Ive played RTS's for quite some years grew up playing AOE 2 and AOM. Thought I'd try this out to see if its any good. I mostly play Beyond all reason now a days, just cant get better unit control than that game personally, it has so many QoL improvements other RTS sadly lack. Played the tutorial, then went to que into a few Multiplayer matches and im getting qued with Lvl 700's and 100 + with a few noobs here and  there. Now granted I watched a few videos before getting into it. I'll just say i got thrown around like a rag doll and dont feel like fighting against people who have been playing this game for 4 years. Personally I'm usta microing 50+ units and this game just feels sloppy to me and just poorly designed. The fact that a few units just shut you down or you get bullied bc people see your rank just poor taste that u cant defend your base less that 5 mins in when you got 3+ players all focusing you and a team not communicating to help. The only thing im glad about is that i didnt play more that 2 hours so ill get a refund lmao. Do not recommend this game at all go play beyond all reason instead of this so much better, its free, has a decent community attached to it as well, and is less slop than this.
Fun and interesting game. I really enjoy the variety between factions.
AOE IV is an amazing refresh to the RTS genre - love the graphics, overall gameplay. It's well put together!

I absolutely loved this game, spent many hours in it.

Then an update corrupted my save file and made me lose HOURS of game.

and apparently the devs know this and expect us to just be ok with it?!

F*CK THIS GAME!
fix your game data retrieving, is always loading and doesn't get the achievement

I really like the game but I am going to recommend against it because this is the best graphical esport RTS and developers are ruining the esport side of it. Please just keep main civs balanced and remove others from PVP.

Broken game. I have been in love with this game for years. I'm lvl 500 so I have clearly played the game a lot. Over the last year or so the game has gotten to be un playable. I stated crashing not stop no matter what the game mode was. Custom games, quick play, ranked, nomad and more. I have never left a review for a game in my life but when a game I love so much becomes un playable I need to let others know not to buy it or any of the dlc's. I have done everything I can think of to mix the issue but it keep crashing.

The fact that the game is not playable deserves nothing but shame.
I love the franchise but not being able to launch the game, even after trying everything possible to fix the issue is just not okay.
Don't buy this game until this is fixed.
This genre has a bad tendency to release the game then chip at you with DLC's. I haven't played in years and fired it up to see 2 new game modes that i cant access without paying more money.

In my opinion its a big downgrade from age of empires 3 .Its very arcade and npcs dont size right with the buildings and if it were to get scaled to a building they would look giant also there are very few choices in npc types or buildings .Now i think the mods are way too simple and most of them are too similar .In conclusion it looks more like a documentary than a game.

They brought the good from most past games, left alot of the bad, and made a unique experience. The game looks great, which is always a plus. A great addition to the franchise, though I'd recommend waiting for a sale to pick up all the DLC. 

Campaigns are well done. A lot of time was put into their delivery.
A fun and fresh take on a classic series! Factions are more in depth and asymmetric than AoE II, which is the closest to in the series to this game.

Age of Empires IV has some good ideas, and I’ll give credit to the built-in mod manager—it’s one of the better parts of the game. Unfortunately, that’s where a lot of the praise ends.

The controls feel clunky and restrictive for a modern RTS, and the co-op experience is incredibly frustrating. My friend and I genuinely spent more time trying to get co-op settings and shared gameplay options to work than we did actually playing the game.

To make things worse, some content feels unnecessarily locked behind DLC, which only adds to the feeling that the base experience is less complete than it should be.

AoE IV isn’t a terrible game, but it’s a frustrating one—full of good ideas held back by clunky design choices, limited co-op options, and systems that seem to fight the player instead of support them.

SI on pouvait mettre une note neutre j'aurais mis neutre mais  n'ayant pas le choix je met rouge.

Niveau solo j'ai vraiment rien à dire sur le jeu que ce soit scénario, campagnes, maps proposés etc.

Si le jeu était solo uniquement je serais satisfait.

J'ai voulu tenter le multi. 
L'écroulement d'un monde.
Tu regardes des vidéos tu te dis que vraiment la majorité des mecs sont pas hyper forts en jeu (j'ai vu des platines fairent des erreurs que je fais jamais)
Le problème du jeu ?

Avant d'avoir un rang tu dois faire 5 games de placement pour juger ton niveau.
Juger selon le jeu c'est toi 0h de multi on te met face à des gogols golds qui suivent le meme build order sur la meme civ qu'ils jouent depuis 2021. ca spam l'armée tu as aucune chance.
Comment en non classé je bat un gold de 1 000h ? Je sais pas qui à fais ce systeme mais çà tue l'envie des gens de faire du multi.

Une fois la terrible douleur des 5 games passés tu te dis "c'est bon je vais affronter des mecs de mon niveaux et le vrai jeu ou j'ai une chance commence.
PERDU ! t'affrontes encore des GOLDS a 1 000h de jeu qui jouent la meme civ en boucle.
Genial....
Comment j'atteint le gold ou simplement je progresse si je tombe sur des mecs qui ont x10 mon skills et experiences ? Big brain le matchmaking....
Et le pire parce que dans une game un mec a surrend a 30 secondes le jeu me considere "silver" pour 1 win...

Oui parce que le matchmaking tu peux le truquer aussi. Y a pleins de mecs qui font expres de surrend pour tomber bas et farmer les noobs et nouveaux. Les devs font rien contre çà, les nouveaux joueurs ca doit pas etre rentable j'imagine....

J'aimerais au moins une fois genre affronter des mecs de mon niveau (bronze/silver) pour juste .... avoir pas l'impression que je peux rien faire ?

Le multi y a rien de ouf. Pas d'éco, pas de macro/micro tu oublies tout.
Comment gagner : produit des troupes en masses et envoient a l'infini chez ton adversaire....
- controle de map
- pas de panique pour toi
- + de villageois
- + de ressources
- + de troupes
A ce niveau la c'est brainrot les mecs et les strats....

J'ai tester une fois la technique et j'ai gagner.... mais aucun plaisir.... ca sert a quoi les BO, micro/macro top 10 plays si çà se resume a rush l'adversaire c'est debile. Autant jouer un starcraft a ce niveau.

Ont va me dire : "va sur discord et trouves des mecs pour jouer" 
Je l'ai fais.
Les gens sotn sympas okay. Mais c'est un peu du faux. Les gens veulent juste s'occuper. Une fois en game peut importe leurs niveaux balec que tu sois nouveau le but est pas de t'apprendre mais te detruire et t'humilier.
J'ai affronter un platine le mec avait full bateaux (archipel) bon ben gg tu peux rien faire.
Toujours le meme discours "moi aussi j'ai perdu beaucoup"
C'est pas une raison parce que toi tu t'es fais bully de bully les autres derriere...vous tuez juste le jeu en degoutant les nouveaux.

Je veux qu'ont m'aident pas qu'ont me rappellent que j'ai aucune chance.
L'humiliation a ete de perdre un 2v1.
Tu te dis ben le mec a jamais fais de classer et tu perds en 2v1 la honte.
A la fin le mec t'avoue qu'il fais des personnalisées depuis un moment et qu'il est coacher. Donc les mecs cachent des informations juste pour avoir leurs games pour win leur ego.

C'est toxique comme environnement. Ok je me fais pas insulter ils sont tous gentils (encore une fois) mais le sourire c'est pour appater le client et lui rouler dessus.

Donc j'ai deux choix : perdre 50 games d'affilées pour devenir brain dead et rouler a mon tour sur du mec qui ont rien demandé pour me sentir mieux ou juste desinstaller le jeu.

Genial
Damn it, it keeps crashing. If it crashes a few more times, I'm going to delete the game.

Not recommended for purchase .
1. NO RANKED SYSTEM ( If anyone leaves from your team whether its first minute or 35 minute doesnt matter you still lose Ranked points)  and no penalty or something similar you can join ranked game again and leave again and you can spam it and NOBODY WILL CARE ABOUT IT. Reached out to support they don't plan to change anything first  they are working on more DLC (THAT MEANS MORE $$) . 
2. there are several cheaters maybe from 40 play 1. 
3.  if you still buy this game play casual.
4. you see chinese , japanese  or any asian names leave game until it starts 
5. there is no leave button from searching so you should quit from the game by alt + f4.
 638,1 Hrs was playing but played 5 match and 2 of them team just leave nobody cares about anything. 
Good luck to anyone who will buy this game be prepared you can spam Ranked , join leave join leave and you wont get any penalties ( like you can't join the match ) but everyone will lose Points who left + who didnt leave but lose.

I think the game, IMO, is almost unplayable as too much going on and no way to just simply pause it to scroll around and move units/see the map etc while on pause. So many (most?) games have this simple feature - even previous games did. Shocking to me that this one does not.

The only quasi "pause" function (on desktop) is using escape which brings up the main menu and you can't scroll to see the field.

As the game has SO much going on (which I think is a great approach but not usable) when like multiple groups are attacking from multiple directions.
I guess, is the game meant to be a chaotic, unmanageable game? Or more a thoughtful, strategy game like the other AOE were? As right now it fails on being strategic and is more for Chaos.

A simple pause, scroll and add commands, IMO, would make this game so much better and help developers increase sales - while benefiting users.

While it's no Age 2, it lives up to the Age of Empires name in my books. Worth playing, maybe wait for sales for the DLC. Crucible mode is a nice hold-out mode.
2 > AoM > 4 > 1 > 3.
Great game, my favorite out of the series. I'm DLC is still coming out for it.
This game is all about History , The story was well narrated and the game play is beautiful, played this for the first time recommend it for sure!.

Just bought the game a month ago, used to be able to play on rog ally with desktop mode now after the update it forces me to play handheld mode which limits the game to 4 person maps. Also the new UI sucks, its the handheld device one. I’m not playing it till the devs do something about this, also a lot of people are complaining about this, update it and make it better or give our money back!
Yes it is very fun and addictive to play, can never get bored of this game
Good game, like AOE 2. But relies too much on DLCs to feel like a complete game.
"Due to a recent update, saved game isn't available" ...seriously....seriously...SERIUSLY

I've been an AOE2 and AOM fan for over twenty years and never really got into this ones mechanics. However, I gave it another chance and I have to say it's growing a lot on me. The campaigns are incredibly well made and the added documentaries are truly amazing. The gameplay is very smooth, every faction feels unique and the visual look great. The only thing I believe is not as good as the rest of the game are the derivate factions. I respect that the team tried doing something new but I think that getting fewer factions than AOE 2 but more unique ones would be better for this installment. Instead of adding more derivate factions add fewer unique factions like Castilla-Aragon, Aztecs and/or Mayans, some Italian states or more African states.

Only 20 hours in but I love it. I played aoe2 a long time ago and never got into it, but aoe4 has brought me back. Multiplayer and campaign are both great. I would highly recommend this game for those who love rts.
A truly classic game which allows me to escape realityfor a while.
Presentation is great, campaign takes you through historical events, plays a lot like Age of Mythology, but weirdly simpler. I like it!

Me and my whole family have played this whole game series for years and aoe4 is one of the best if not the best installment in the whole series. We have played this game since the release. it is my favorite aoe game so far.
They destroy your saved games when there is an update.  I would not recommend this game if you play solo  and rely on saved games.
I'm exclusive single-player, and so far the campaign has been great. I Love the documental-like videos you unlock after each mission.

I have not put in the hours in Age of Empires IV as i have in previous decades with its predecessors, but its looking like its getting there. Sat too long but finally got to playing some. 4k gaming is a nice step up, though it seems I cant zoom out as far as in previous versions.

It's not awful, it's a solid improvement over AoE3 and (imo) 2, but the single player game is very limited and weak.  The different factions have probably the most diversity they've had in an AoE title which is pretty cool, but it's still far less diversity than SC2 or even AoM if that's something you value. Unless you're big into AoE 1v1 laddering I don't think I'd get this for more than $20.
Pretty balance game with nice graphycs , the campaign was a blast to run and multiplayer is not bad at all.Worth the money spent on it,

Love the game and series, spent many hours enjoying it, but the multiplayer delay completely ruins it for me. Even on good internet with players nearby, unit commands feel noticeably delayed. Single-player is fantastic, but the multiplayer responsiveness is frustrating enough that i refuse to play it.
Not as close as the old legacy titles holds the worth but nevertheless one of the best RTS games for me.

Great entry to AoE. It's a stripped down version of AoE 2. Building is streamlined, faster combat, smaller scale battles, and less complex maps. Will say this is the first game I feel like they got ship combat right.

This game is great! Played AOE growing up and with these new graphics and civ's it just feels great playing and experiencing these once great civilizations. 

I play both competitively and casually finding them both enjoyable and tough depending on how you set up the games. 

If you're feeling casual, campaign's are fun as well as simple skirmish's vs AI, teams, or one v one. 
Ranked matches can be a challenge but such a rewarding rush when you crush and conquer an opponent.  

If you are new to RTS games the learning curve is quite strong but there are so many tutorials and info online/youtube that can get you all that you need to know to get a grip on the mechanics.

All in all IMO the different Civs are fun and I enjoyed learning them all and getting them down enough to feel confident in different build orders and land adaptation and it all just comes to life when you have a massive civilization and a world-shaking army. 

Cant WAIT for more Civs!

This Game Is amazing, if your looking for an, War simulator this game is the one. the controls are easy to learn and there is a variety of empires you can chose from, (Even In the Standerd game) And it is really fun to play with the storys and stuff, I advise you to also buy the dlcs if you want.
Nice game. Quite expensive DLCs and demanding to run..

The singleplayer has great production quality and is a smooth entry into the game and its mechanics. If you buy RTS games mostly for their campaigns, then this one is fine from a gameplay standpoint. Missions are on rails most of the time with not a lot of interesting decision making or macro play. As a journey through key battles in history, though, its is fantastic. Base game is full of fun cutscenes and videos that show how new medieval technology shake the power dynamics of the time and set the scene for upcoming missions. Had fun, but definitely not a campaign I would replay.

For multiplayer enjoyers, this is genuinely the best 1v1 pvp in all of RTS (imo obv). Civs are different varied - both visually and in gameplay.
Map is full of strategic objectives that make playing the map fun and important. Scouting is well balanced, and it is hard to get cheesed if you do a decent job at scouting (I'm looking at you, starcraft 2). Faster than Aoe2 but not zoomer fast. Uncs rise up

Good game, but unfortunately no innovation at all. Who likes AOE will like this game. But how can save games be broken after an update? - I dont like!!!!!!!!!!! This fact almost makes me not recommend this game. And why the F cant i save my custom multiplayer game vs AI!?
Great gameplay and runs well even with huge numbers of units
I like this game because this features a lot of strategy and building, but it takes a long time to get good at it.

Love this game, Played Age of empires 2 and mythology when i was younger. I have brought all the DE as they were released. But found myself enjoying and playing AoE 4 the most. Graphics are great, love the gameplay. I have all the DLC's each brought something new and interesting. Hope these continue and looking forward to potential new AOE if that happens. Takes some time to really learn all the Civs and strategies but so much fun.
I have enjoyed the context of the game and the overall design of the game. It is fun and it makes you think.

This game is honestly a really great RTS. At first it can feel a bit overwhelming and even frustrating because you actually need time and practice to get decent at it. But once you start practicing and learn every civilizations it gets really fun and addictive.

You constantly have to multitask: economy, army,  planning all at once and thats what makes it really satisfying when you start getting better at it.

Its not perfect though. In particular, I think the history of Rus is not clearly separated from the Moscow state even though historically they are different entities with different development paths and the connection between them is more complex. I think they should be presented as separate historical developments.
Also matchmaking can sometimes put you against much higher ranked and more experienced players. Its not really a problem but in some matches it can be pretty hard to compete and you definitely feel the skill gap.

But overall if you dont get too stuck on those things the game is really enjoyable
I liked the gameplay and the storyline although the storyline campaign is very short.
The issue of disconnecting from and then reconnecting to the game must be resolved
New players will have a steep learning curve into this RTS game but when u get the hang of it is kinda rewarding. DLC civs are great imo 8/10.

So many updates yet the mods have not been fixed. For me personally, the mods are what drives me to play further taking it as extra content and it makes the game much more fun. Most mods dont work as they are incompatible with new updates. AoE 2 had the best mods. please balance it. Game is amazing.
Good game. Plays faster and more asymmetrically than AOE2.
It's a fun game as you can see by my hours played. But started to get old and the maps are way to small for a good long romp.
really fun RTS, especially for team games with friends. Easy to get into for new players, great campaign content. Would be even better if the game had a reconnect feature for disconnected matches

I love this game and I spent countless hours on it, but the bugs are starting worse and worse.
My single player matches are never recorded for some reason, and I am forced to start a multiplayer match with AI in order to be recorded.

I played aoe2 as a kid growing up, and I played it a few additional times in college and since then. AOE4 is just a lovely game. All the civilizations feel very different and unique, and the singleplayer modes are fun as well if MP is not what you're looking for. I finally got back into MP, and excited to continue playing. Just wish I wouldn't have waited 10+ seasons to get into it again, but what can ya do.

I highly recommend this game to all gamers who love RTS games. The most disappointing thing in the game is that the DLCs are very expensive, and civilizations (and their variants) are 100% dependent on buying DLCs. I prefer that choosing civilizations be independent. It feels a little pay-to-win to me.
Latest patch is worst. Unable to connect to server. Getting marked as game lost and rank getting affected
Excellent game! A little mix of CIV and Total War. If you like either of those games, check this is out!

Prior to this latest May 2026 patch, the game worked fine (granted, only played about 6 hours as it was a bundle special w/ the other AoE franchise games).

It now crashes shortly after the game launches (within 3 - 5 minutes). 

reliccardinal.exe is also trying to use explorer.exe to load or alter kernel32.dll. The way it's using explorer.exe would grant it full rights on the system. I'm not sure what it's doing there, but none of my other games have ever attempted anything like this.

I don't trust this game and neither should you.

Crashes incessantly, saving games does nothing as I assume the files themselves have a problem. I do not use mods or play online, only AI skirmish and campaign. The game is currently unplayable. Before the new Jin Dynasty update it crashed every now and again, now it is constant.
Their latest patch broke this game. I've had to download more than 50 gigs' worth of the same install and then spend hours for it to patch the files.
Nostalgic and overall mentally stimulating and engaging
Great game very addicting, The devs are always updating. What was strong is now weak. I have played a little bit.
Awesome Strategy Game. More fun and lots to learn. Little confused with Technology when compared to AOE 3, but once we learn it is fun for sure
Excellent RTS game.  Many things from AoE II corrected.  Still, the AoE II title still carries its own charm, like quick walling, which is fun and people enjoy.  I get it.
A fantastic diversity of units makes this a game of diverse strategy.

Awesome game that has recently been updated to crash in FFAs. Audio glitches to deafening levels following by a game crash. Seems to correlate when population gets to 400+ (total visible). Can't recommend this game until this bug is fixed

don't listen to what other reviews say, this game is still the best RTS in our heart. New fun mechanics, beautiful music and language voiceline, bunch of unique units, all and you can enjoy with your friends, or play with bots.... Noone is forcing you to play ranked or try hard something... just enjoy your game after a long day of work
Great fun and a lot of strategy

Brilliant game ! I played my first Ages of empire game 25 yrs ago -- at that time played crazy, full on mode for hours together -- fast track now, the latest versions still got the vibe and sometimes I play for straight 7hrs ! If you like strategy games, this is the best - haven't found another game equivalent to this one yet !

This is a great RTS game. My only wish is that the devs would focus on quality over quantity and release new fully-fledged civs like Japanese and Byzantines in the past rather than multiple uninspired variants in the last few DLCs.
realy good game hase meny difrent things and stratagys to tray out
One of the best modern strategies.. will be better to add napoleonic warfare - uniforms, arms and soliders
Good strategy game with different types of campaigns. Or create your own. Difficulty settings make it as easy or as hard as you want.

Great Game to spend time and enjoy.
mix of nostaliga time playing AoE 1 with Graphics/ Gaming upgrate plus Historical accuracy.... niceone..

This game is THE premier RTS available to fans of the genre right now. The diversity of civilizations and gameplay styles is amazing. The attention to detail on the history of (almost) every civ and variant is commendable. The music is immersive, and now that I've finally upgraded to an overkill desktop after playing this on my laptop for 3+ years, I can confidently say the graphics are beautiful in 4k. 

The balance for multiplayer is tweaked every couple months and while never perfect, the devs are clearly committed to taking in feedback and making positive changes to support as many players as possible. If you like turtling and teching up, greedily booming, or full baboon all-in attacks, this game has a civ you can learn and find success with. 

If you're like me, and 30+ and miss the heyday of Starcraft, Warcraft 3, and other classics that we never realized we're so lucky to be inundated with, I think this is the best way to scratch the RTS itch and improve in a deeply complex and rewarding game. It's not a perfect game, but I can't recommend it enough.

The match making process of this game is horrible. Players who are just starting to play ranked or even quick matches and trying to progress are being matched with insanely high level of players. I myself don't have a super high level (its roughly around 70), and yet the matchups I kept on getting were all of 500+ (sometimes even 1000+) level players. Its not just one or two matches, almost 98% of matches being played were like that. This just adds on to the frustration and defeats any sense of purpose of trying to play this game! It could be a fun game, BUT, such a pathetic matchmaking process just makes zero sense!
If you're already a top tier player, this game will seem amazing, but, if you are new, there can't be a more frustrating game (since you'll only loose).

Huge shoutout to the Age of Empires IV dev team — absolutely masterful work. You've managed to lock me out of online play for three days straight. I've tried literally everything: restarting the game, restarting my PC, verifying game files, even spinning up a VPN node in Seoul... nothing. The game still dumps me into offline mode the moment I launch it, even though my internet is 100% rock solid.
700+ hours in this game btw, so I'm not just some random tourist. At this point, I genuinely hope you keep up this momentum. Do the entire RTS community a favour and make this game completely disappear from the scene — that would honestly be your greatest achievement. Legends. 👏

Update: Servers are back, so as is tradition, I’m flipping my review back to positive. Good job, devs. Seriously, keep it up — like, actually keep the servers alive this time. I have the most unhinged love-hate relationship with this team. ❤️🤬 See you on the ladder… until the next mental breakdown.
